title: trisetum pratense
intro: Trisetum pratense, also known as Green Oat-grass or False Oat-grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Trisetum pratense include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. Trisetum pratense is typically harvested in the summer months and can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties.
